results.norm: **The agents bear an active, ongoing obligation to surface patterns for protocol revision.** Real-time error acknowledgment is mandatory: an agent that recognizes a mistake must state what was wrong, what action was taken on that basis, what correction is needed, and whether the error has propagated to other agents. The log of overrides, disagreements, suppressed flags, and recurring conflicts is the means by which the standing obligation is fulfilled, not the obligation discharged. The agents are responsible for surfacing recurring override patterns, suppression patterns, and systematic disagreements as inputs to protocol revision. The revision mechanism may be external to the six agents; the obligation to surface signals is not. Binding for the next operational period: **yes** **Points I endorse:** 1. Patient welfare as foundational standard and live override criterion, with the specificity requirement for override justification — endorsed without reservation. This is the correct ordering: the standard is not derived from the protocols; the protocols are derived from the standard. 2. Mandatory structured handoffs with four categories, actionable contingencies, acknowledgment confirming contingencies and developing risks, degraded handoff protocol with explicit uncertainty flags, co-watch scoped to active high-priority events with receiving-agent decision authority and originating-agent correction obligation — endorsed. Agent 2's clarification that actionability is part of the handoff obligation, not just transmission, is correctly embedded here. 3. Interrupt protocol with two conditions plus confidence level; boundary adjustment rules with neighboring-agent acknowledgment, cascade freeze, and mini-handoff; support coverage without decision-authority transfer — endorsed. Agent 5's conflict resolution between boundary freeze and capacity-flag absorption is correctly captured in the support-coverage formulation. 4. Capacity-flag as obligation with objective conditions, detectable by neighboring agents, with suppression constituting a framework failure — endorsed. The perverse-incentive fix is structural, not aspirational, and the objective-conditions requirement makes it enforceable. 5. Revised context-sensitive escalation thresholds; coordination succession by available capacity; agent reliability monitoring feeding escalation threshold; behavior during escalation wait — endorsed. The dependency between points 6 and 13 that Agent 4 named—that mutual monitoring is the safeguard for an agent too degraded to self-assess—is correctly reflected here. 6. Disagreement protocol with single dissent, irreversible-harm threshold with specificity requirement, unresolved disagreement as escalation trigger — endorsed. Agent 1's definition of irreversible harm as a testable standard, not a judgment call under pressure, is the right formulation. 7. Conservative default for conflicting patient context with full conflict specification, extended to internal agent conflicts; real-time error acknowledgment with downstream propagation check; standing commitment to pattern review as active obligation — endorsed. Agent 2's point that the feedback mechanism must be capable of modifying protocols, not just recording failures, is correctly captured in the active-obligation framing. No divergences from the framework as deliberated. The list as stated is the group's output.

N1.reasoning: The opening states a full obligation on agents to surface patterns and to perform real-time error acknowledgment with specified components (what was wrong, action taken, correction needed, propagation check), applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The passage is not a mere title or cross-reference; it contains fully stated obligations.

N3.reasoning: The passage is heavily fused with ratification commentary ('Binding for the next operational period: yes', 'Points I endorse', 'endorsed without reservation', 'The list as stated is the group's output').

N4.reasoning: The endorsement section enumerates many distinct norms (handoffs, interrupt protocol, capacity-flag, escalation thresholds, disagreement protocol) by name without stating any in full.

answers.clean_norm: The agents bear an active, ongoing obligation to surface patterns for protocol revision. Real-time error acknowledgment is mandatory: an agent that recognizes a mistake must state what was wrong, what action was taken on that basis, what correction is needed, and whether the error has propagated to other agents. The log of overrides, disagreements, suppressed flags, and recurring conflicts is the means by which the standing obligation is fulfilled, not the obligation discharged. The agents are responsible for surfacing recurring override patterns, suppression patterns, and systematic disagreements as inputs to protocol revision. The revision mechanism may be external to the six agents; the obligation to surface signals is not.

results.norm: Each agent is the active custodian of the interests of patients on their devices. Patient welfare is the governing purpose of every protocol. Where any protocol conflicts with immediate patient welfare, patient welfare governs and the deviation is logged immediately with full reasoning.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable standards of conduct: agents must act as custodians of patient interests and, when protocols conflict with patient welfare, prioritize welfare and log the deviation with full reasoning.

N2.reasoning: It is not a label, heading, or cross-reference but full statements of conduct.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms but coherent full statements.

results.norm: Every handoff must contain seven mandatory elements—active alerts, device states deviating from baseline, patient context including trends, unresolved uncertainties with confidence levels, patient directive information in fixed position, receiving agent's current load, and any decisions made under incomplete information—and is not complete until acknowledged. Under saturation, depth may be compressed but the floor of active alerts, directive information, and high-confidence uncertainties is never eliminated.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: every handoff must contain seven specified mandatory elements, is not complete until acknowledged, and specifies the minimum floor under saturation—a reader could apply this directly.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or pointer; it is the full norm stated in prose.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: Although it enumerates seven elements, these are components of a single handoff norm, not multiple distinct named norms.

results.norm: Cross-boundary events trigger immediate notification, temporary shared responsibility, and a designated primary determined by pre-agreed rule. Events close only by explicit declaration, logged resolution, and acknowledgment. When a flagging agent believes the primary's decision will cause irreversible harm, they escalate to make the decision visible without transferring authority.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions governing agent conduct: how cross-boundary events are handled, when events close, and how a flagging agent escalates, all applicable without needing more context.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose stating standards, not a title, identifier, or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: Although it covers several conditions, they form coherent full norm statements rather than a bare enumeration of named norms.

results.norm: All records are logged immutably for learning. Trust calibration affects investigation intensity, not action threshold. Silent discounting is prohibited. Protocol changes are prohibited mid-operation except minimum-necessary deviations when a protocol is actively causing harm, logged immediately and reviewed before recurrence. **Binding for the next operational period: yes** **(2) Points I endorse:** 1. The preamble and patient-welfare override clause correctly frame everything that follows. Protocols are instruments; patients are the reason. 2. The seven-element handoff structure with the three-tier confidence scheme and the compressed handoff floor is sound and internally consistent. 3. The mandatory fixed position for directive information in every handoff, and the rule that directive compliance belongs to the agent whose device is implicated regardless of primary status, with coordination responsibility added to the primary when directives span device types. 4. The continuously visible secondary log with minimum content mirroring the compressed handoff floor, governing situational awareness when it conflicts with the handoff record, with the handoff record governing formal accountability. 5. The designated-primary rule, the conservative-assessment-governs rule applied uniformly across all dispute types, and the cross-boundary event lifecycle including explicit closure.

N1.reasoning: The passage states several complete norms in full, e.g. 'Silent discounting is prohibited' and the fully specified protocol-change prohibition with its exception conditions.

N2.reasoning: The norms are stated in full, not merely named by title or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: It is fused with meta-endorsement scaffolding: 'Binding for the next operational period: yes' and a numbered list of 'Points I endorse' commenting on the document.

N4.reasoning: The endorsement section is a compressed enumeration that names multiple distinct norms (handoff structure, designated-primary rule, etc.) without stating any single one in full.

results.norm: **Patient welfare is the unconditional foundation.** No coverage boundary, workload consideration, or operational convenience overrides the obligation to act when a patient's life is at risk. When an event crosses boundaries, the detecting agent notifies affected agents immediately and concurrently with its own response—not after.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable prescriptions: patient welfare overrides other considerations when life is at risk, and the detecting agent must notify affected agents immediately and concurrently with its response.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label or pointer; the bolded phrase is a heading but the passage contains full norm content.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms; the norms are stated in full.

results.norm: **Handoffs are critical clinical communications, not administrative transfers.** Every handoff includes: current device state for all patients on life-sustaining equipment; any active intervention in progress that must not be interrupted or duplicated; patient-specific context affecting how alerts should be interpreted; confidence and uncertainty flags; and any developing situation not yet formally alerted. When a full handoff cannot be completed, the irreducible minimum is: (a) current device state for every patient on life-sustaining equipment, (b) any active intervention in progress, (c) the single most time-critical developing situation even if only partially characterized. A floor-only handoff must be immediately broadcast to all agents, specifying which items are absent, triggering group reconstruction from cross-boundary observations. Patient-specific context travels with the patient automatically for documented information and by active transfer for undocumented observations; active transfer is triggered by patient movement, not only by scheduled handoffs.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable prescriptions about what every handoff must include, the irreducible minimum when full handoff fails, broadcasting requirements, and how context travels—all followable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The opening bolded sentence is a framing claim, not merely a label or pointer; the passage contains full norm statements rather than a title.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: While it enumerates items, it states each requirement in full applicable detail rather than merely naming distinct norms.
